title: Human genetics of RhoA–ROCK expression highlights limits of expression-based validation for an intracellular kinase target
description: Code, processed inputs, result tables, and figures accompanying the study "Human genetics of RhoA–ROCK expression highlights limits of expression-based validation for an intracellular kinase target" (Kim & Kim). We test whether genetically proxied expression of the RhoA–ROCK pathway (ROCK2, ROCK1, RHOA) is causally associated with cardiometabolic and fibrosis/liver outcomes, using two-sample cis-eQTL Mendelian randomization and Bayesian colocalization (single-variant coloc.abf and multi-signal coloc.susie), benchmarked against tissue-appropriate positive controls. Main finding: no robust evidence that constitutive ROCK-pathway expression affects these traits (all SuSiE-evaluable PP.H4 < 0.11); type 2 diabetes null across European, Finnish, and trans-ancestry analyses; positive controls recovered (KCNJ11 to T2D PP4 = 0.89; liver SORT1 to LDL PP4 = 1.00); coloc.abf and coloc.susie concordant. The null is interpreted as a limit of expression-based genetic validation for an intracellular, activity-dependent kinase target. Contents: Python MR / coloc.abf pipeline; R coloc.susie inputs and runner; processed instruments and cis-region extracts; per-locus SuSiE inputs (z/beta + 1000G EUR LD); result tables; and code to regenerate all figures. Raw summary statistics are publicly available from the original consortia (eQTLGen, GTEx / eQTL Catalogue, GWAS Catalog, MAGIC, GLGC, FinnGen R12, DIAMANTE, Genebass, 1000 Genomes); see the repository data/README. Repository: https://github.com/charliekim97/rock-pathway-mr

creators.name: Kim, Sangeon Charles
